narrative.summary: The anchor restaurant of Ridgewood's food scene. Mediterranean-leaning, seasonal, 4.5★ (1789 reviews). TimeOut and every neighborhood guide names it as the must-eat in the area. Literally in Dessa's neighborhood. $$. Strong Williamsburg–Bushwick–Ridgewood corridor fit with the design-forward food/art energy John tracks.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Delicious delicious delicious. I’m so glad we decided to dine here on our trip. The food was phenomenal. I don’t think there is a bad thing on the menu. The tortellini and the lasagna were so good. The fish was fresh and so yummy. The polenta bread with the chili butter was so good and the mortadella was delish as well."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I highly highly recommend the leafy greens and herb salad, calabrian chili wood fired polenta bread combined with the house made mortadella, two sheet lasagna, and lastly, the littleneck clam pasta. I personally loved the crispy potatoes "war style" but do think the pungent lemongrass dip can be polarizing.
The fluke crudo was so unfresh that it made me lose my usually robust appetite (and taste-wise was very disappointing compared to other crudos), but the server was hospitable and took it off the cheque. The pork t-bone was tasty but not as remarkable as the other dishes.
Rolo's is very ingredient-forward and works with flavors in a unique way.

Minus the fluke crudo, 5/5 dining experience."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Made reservations walked in and was pleasantly surprised..this is a busy establishment reservations are recommended..Natalie who was our waitress was an absolute doll. So informative with a wealth of knowledge about the food. The food hands down was a ten out of ten. We ordered the crispy potatoes for the table, yum and yum. A second order was made immediately don't judge. I ordered the eggs with the polenta bread sooo good.my friend ordered the French toast which was to die for..need I say more. The food is very filling and so very delicious."
